market_note: Stablecoin on-chain volume has hit $7.5T with market cap at $316.7B, as GENIUS Act capital, reserve and licensing rules for issuers were finalized on July 18.

stablecoin_items.headline: GENIUS Act stablecoin rulemaking deadline lands
stablecoin_items.summary: Six federal agencies finalized capital, reserve, and licensing rules for USDC, USDT and other issuers on July 18, setting the regulatory bar for compliant stablecoins.

stablecoin_items.headline: Tether risks USDT delisting from US exchanges
stablecoin_items.summary: About 25% of USDT reserves remain non-compliant with GENIUS Act cash-and-Treasuries requirements; full delisting from US exchanges looms by 2028 if unresolved.

stablecoin_items.headline: USDC overtakes USDT in transaction volume
stablecoin_items.summary: Circle's USDC captured ~70% of adjusted stablecoin transaction volume in H1 2026 versus Tether's ~25%, aided by Circle's new OCC national trust bank approval.

hack_items.headline: Wanchain Cardano-BNB bridge drained of $10M
hack_items.summary: Attackers stole roughly 515.2M NIGHT tokens (~$10M) from Wanchain's bridge treasury in four transactions over an eight-minute window on July 21.

hack_items.headline: Ostium oracle exploit nets $24M
hack_items.summary: A compromised oracle private key let an attacker sign future-dated fake price reports, triggering a fraudulent ~$24M USDC payout from Ostium's RWA perps vault on Arbitrum.

hack_items.headline: 'Ill Bloom' vulnerability drains over $5M from wallets
hack_items.summary: A newly disclosed exploit chain dubbed 'Ill Bloom' has been used to siphon more than $5 million from cryptocurrency wallets in the past week.

hack_items.headline: Drift Protocol hit for $286M in suspected DPRK attack
hack_items.summary: Blockchain analytics firm Elliptic linked a $286M exploit of Solana-based Drift Protocol to North Korea-affiliated threat actors, one of 2026's largest DeFi hacks.
